danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard in intestinal passages; instant threat requiring avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: Spikes and pointed obstacles embedded in terrain
danger_signals.meaning: Contact damage; deflates Jim's balloon head form, causing descent penalty

danger_signals.visual_form: Projectiles or melee strikes from antagonist sprite
danger_signals.meaning: Active enemy aggression during vulnerable states (floating head level)

danger_signals.visual_form: Embedded dangerous elements in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental damage; forces careful navigation through confined spaces

safety_signals.visual_form: Green slimy character sprite following Jim
safety_signals.meaning: Utility tool for adhesion mechanics; enables safe descent via parachute function

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible sprites increasing suit power to 200%
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability or damage resistance state

safety_signals.visual_form: Gun sprites with distinct visual designs (auto-aiming gun, Barn Blaster)
safety_signals.meaning: Offensive capability expansion; removes threats via ranged elimination

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Earthworm silhouette; heroic platformer protagonist iconography
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Levels themed around Jim's journey; anthropomorphic presentation contrasts with hostile environments

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crow character design; final boss identity
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Appears in final race-format level 'See Jim Run Run Jim Run'
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Associated with kidnapping narrative; Princess What's-Her-Name as objective marker of conflict
Balloon-themed level environment; elevated threat during Jim's vulnerable floating state
Associated with 'The Flyin' King' isometric level; requires balloon-bomb defense mechanic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Colorful level geometry with run-and-gun layout; multiple gun types visible as pickups
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jim moves horizontally; standard shooting and jumping controls; Snott follows for adhesion assistance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Interior flesh-colored passages; organic, tunnel-like corridors with embedded hazards
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced perspective navigation; visual disguise as blind cave salamander changes Jim's sprite appearance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: HUD-style multiple choice interface; sudden shift from level geography to abstract quiz format
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Puzzle solving replaces action; nonsensical questions with no logically correct answers signal absurdist tone

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jim's head rendered as balloon sprite; upward floating trajectory; sharp objects appear as visible threats
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vertical movement replaces horizontal; collision with spikes causes descent reset; Evil the Cat becomes active threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Isometric camera angle; Jim on pocket rocket; balloon-with-bomb as objective visual element
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different control scheme; defensive mechanics required; balloon protection from enemies becomes primary task

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Race finale (See Jim Run Run Jim Run level)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Obstacle course layout; Psy-Crow sprite racing alongside Jim
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Competitive navigation; jumping and avoidance focused; non-combat final encounter emphasizes speed over violence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All characters (Jim, Princess, Psy-Crow) revealed as cows; mundane livestock sprites replace heroic designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game-world collapse; narrative undercuts entire adventure with absurdist reveal; eliminates faction distinctions

environmental_storytelling.example: 'Villi People' = intestinal digestive system; 'Inflated Head' = balloon vulnerability; 'Level Ate' = homophone wordplay on 'ate' (eating/consumption theme); 'See Jim Run Run Jim Run' = repetitive chase finale

environmental_storytelling.technique: Gameplay mechanic shift as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Transition from platforming to trivia signals interior environment logic; isometric shift to 'The Flyin' King' indicates alien/fantastical space; upward-only movement in 'Inflated Head' communicates balloon physics as environmental law
Jim disguises as blind cave salamander in biological level; changes sprite appearance to match environment expectations; signals adaptation to hostile ecosystem
Exploding sheep in intestinal passages; spikes embedded in organic tissue; Major Mucus boss design reflects slimy/biological theme; all creatures match grotesque/absurdist aesthetic

Slimy green surfaces (Snott adhesion points) signal utility/safety in biological contexts; contrasting background art across platform versions suggests world instability; final cow reveal strips heroic color coding

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves horizontally; initiates gun fire; collects power-ups; uses Snott for slimy surface adhesion
Floats upward uncontrollably; limited lateral movement; descends upon spike contact; passive dodging replaces active combat
Controls balloon-bomb trajectory; maintains defensive formation against Major Mucus attacks; shifts from protagonist to escort-mechanic role
Trails Jim through levels; becomes interactive tool when player interacts with slimy surfaces; functions as parachute upon command
Launches projectile attacks; targets Jim's balloon-head vulnerability; becomes irrelevant once level environment changes
Moves horizontally in parallel with Jim; responds to obstacle course; represents narrative antagonism without direct violence
Patrol defined paths; fire projectiles; eliminated via Jim's weapons; provide target practice for gun variety (auto-aiming, Barn Blaster screen-clear)

notes: Earthworm Jim 2 communicates world-state primarily through radical gameplay shifts rather than traditional visual design. The game uses platform diversity as narrative language: each level represents a distinct 'world' with unique physics, camera angles, and threat profiles. The ending revelation (all characters as cows) suggests that visual identity and faction markers are unreliable within this paradigm—the game undermines its own visual communication system as a meta-commentary on platformer conventions. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on absurdist level design (exploding sheep, trivia interruptions, balloon physics) rather than diegetic world-building. Manifestation is deliberately surreal and context-specific rather than globally consistent.
